title: Canopy — User Guide
lede: A macOS development tool for visualizing and analyzing SwiftUI View hierarchies
start_sub: Analyze your SwiftUI project in 3 simple steps

steps.p: Press <kbd>⌘ O</kbd> to select your project folder. Canopy automatically detects .swift files and begins AST analysis.
The left panel shows your file list, the center displays the View tree. Click a node to see its source code highlighted in the right panel.
The bottom panel shows quality scores, dependency graphs, and statistics. Click a grade badge to see the complexity breakdown.

start_tip: Canopy is read-only. It never modifies or deletes any of your project's code. Use it with confidence.

features.p: Visualize SwiftUI View hierarchies as a tree structure. Detects nested Views, @ViewBuilder, and View functions in extensions.
Assigns A through F grades to each View. Evaluates complexity, nesting depth, State count, body weight, and modifier order.
Visualize dependencies between Views. Detect circular references, find isolated Views, and trace State-to-Binding flow.
Detects @State, @Binding, @Environment, and @Observable properties. Warns about missing private modifiers and excessive State usage.
Color-coded Swift code display. Highlights lines corresponding to View nodes. Click code to reverse-lookup in the tree.
Click the hammer icon to open the corresponding line directly in Xcode (via xed --line).
Define custom rules with .canopy-rules.json. Share naming conventions, State limits, and nesting depth restrictions across your team.
Add custom rules, exporters, and analyzers as plugins. Browse and install from the plugin catalog.

panels.p: List of analyzed Swift files. Each file shows a grade badge. Click to select.
View hierarchy structure. Shows breadcrumbs, Peek Body (body preview), and Code Vision metrics.
Line-numbered code display. Drag line numbers to select ranges, then copy with ⌘C. Click code to reverse-lookup in the tree.
Three tabs: Quality, Dependencies, Statistics. Resizable and collapsible. Click grade badges for detail popovers.

legend_sub: Meaning of each element shown in the tree and file list

legend_badges.v: Layout badges — HStack / VStack / ZStack / ScrollView / List / Form / Grid
Modifier count badge — number of .modifiers on the node
Peek Body — right-click to expand body preview
Line number — position in source code
Updated within 1 hour (shown in file list)
Updated within 24 hours (shown in file list)
Grade badge — click to view complexity breakdown

export_sub: Output analysis results in various formats
export_tip: Access all export formats from the menu bar under "Project → Export".
cli_sub: Headless execution for CI/CD pipeline integration
cli_c1: Output analysis results in JSON format
cli_setup: Before using the CLI, open the project folder in Canopy (GUI) once. Access permissions are saved, and you can analyze from the CLI from then on.
cli_ci: Upload SARIF output to GitHub Actions to display quality warnings inline on pull requests.
